title: Matelea calcicola
intro: Matelea calcicola, also known as the limestone milkvine, is a perennial vine species in the Apocynaceae family native to the Caribbean region. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, and its extracts have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which can be beneficial in treating various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Matelea calcicola include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Matelea calcicola is traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its preparations include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory problems, as well as to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
